2018-05-25.abstract: Fifteen were wounded, three critically, in the blast just west of Toronto. Officials said there was no immediate indication of terrorism or a hate crime.

2018-05-25.abstract: The number of Indigenous Australian juveniles in detention or on parole is increasing, even as the total number of children accused of crimes is decreasing.

2018-05-25.title: In Malaysia, Calls for New Leaders to Revisit Old Abductions and Deaths
2018-05-25.abstract: The new government is being urged to re-examine mysterious abductions, deaths of prisoners in custody and politically charged murders.

2018-05-26.title: North Korea Blew Up Its Nuclear Test Site. Here’s What the Videos Show.
2018-05-26.abstract: Foreign journalists were given access to the facility in Punggye-ri, and witnessed the destruction of tunnel entrances and buildings in May. We took a closer look.

2018-05-26.title: Keeping Bohemian as Real Estate Prices Soar: The Canada Letter
2018-05-26.abstract: The Times’s Team Canada gets another member, the pressures on a historic neighborhood and buildings that change cities.

2018-05-26.title: Keeping Summit Hopes Alive Suggests Kim Jong-un May Need a Deal
2018-05-26.abstract: When President Trump canceled their meeting, Mr. Kim’s bid to ease sanctions and rebuild the North Korean economy was thrown into doubt.

2018-05-26.title: She Married 3 Brothers in Family Torn by War
2018-05-26.abstract: Khadija’s fate, at 18, has been to be widowed twice and passed down through a family deep in Taliban territory. “I cannot talk about my dreams,” she says.

2018-05-26.title: North Korea Willing to Talk About ‘Complete Denuclearization’
2018-05-26.abstract: Seeking to salvage a landmark meeting with President Trump, Kim Jong-un told South Korea that he is willing to discuss abandoning his arsenal.

2018-05-27.title: Drought Adds to Woes of Afghanistan, in Grips of a Raging War
2018-05-27.abstract: Severe shortages of rainfall across the country could leave up to two million more people without food, the United Nations warned.

2018-05-27.title: A One-Eyed Québécois ‘Rambo’ Captures Imaginations in Canada
2018-05-27.abstract: The superhuman wartime exploits of Léo Major, a Quebec soldier, are inspiring a new generation of Canadians. “If he were American, there would’ve been a dozen films about him by now,” says his son.

2018-05-27.title: At Least 4 Russians Killed in Syria in Firefight With ISIS
2018-05-27.abstract: The clash in Deir al-Zour left 43 militants from the Islamic State dead, the Russian Defense Ministry said.

2018-05-27.title: China Warns 2 American Warships in South China Sea
2018-05-27.abstract: The country’s defense ministry said it had confronted the U.S. warships, which passed through waters near the Paracel Islands that China claims as its own.

2018-05-27.title: Russian City’s Dazzling New Soccer Stadium Outshines Its Team
2018-05-27.abstract: An anticorruption group says World Cup stadium construction became another means for the Kremlin to reward favored oligarchs.

2018-05-28.title: In Colombia, Far-Right and Hard-Left Candidates Will Vie for Presidency
2018-05-28.abstract: A conservative and a former leftist rebel came out ahead in the first-round presidential vote Sunday, setting the stage for a divisive final election.

2018-05-28.title: In Britain, Austerity Is Changing Everything
2018-05-28.abstract: After eight years of budget cutting, Britain is looking less like the rest of Europe and more like the United States, with a shrinking welfare state and spreading poverty.

2018-05-28.title: Australia’s New Rule for Asylum Seekers Makes Ramadan Even Harder
2018-05-28.abstract: The agency that oversees facilities where asylum seekers are held said it banned unpackaged food from outside during the Muslim holy month because of safety concerns.

2018-05-28.title: Paris ‘Spider-Man’ Saves Young Boy. Cue Debate on Migrants.
2018-05-28.abstract: Mamoudou Gassama, from Mali, crawled up several balconies to pull the child to safety. The French president said Mr. Gassama would be granted legal status.

2018-05-28.title: A World War II Mystery Is Solved, and Emotions Flood In
2018-05-28.abstract: The discovery of a bomber shot down 74 years ago has helped a California family find closure, and shows how new technologies are locating those who were long lost.

2018-05-29.title: Roman Abramovich, After Visa Troubles in Britain, Surfaces in Israel
2018-05-29.abstract: The owner of the Chelsea soccer club was encountering delays in renewing his U.K. visa amid worsening tensions between Britain and Russia.

2018-05-29.title: The Case of the Disappearing Athletes
2018-05-29.abstract: The 250 athletes and officials who went missing from this year’s Commonwealth Games have reignited debate about Australia’s immigration policies.

2018-05-29.title: Top Aide to Kim Jong-un Is Bound for U.S., Trump Says
2018-05-29.abstract: The negotiator Kim Yong-chol would be the highest-ranking North Korean to set foot on American soil since 2000.

2018-05-29.title: A Drink in a Bar, a Dip in the Tigris: Mosul Returns to Life
2018-05-29.abstract: Nine months after the Islamic State was driven from Mosul, the Iraqi city is showing new life and energy, though many reminders of the war remain.

2018-05-29.title: Gaza Militants Barrage Israel With Mortars and Rockets
2018-05-29.abstract: Israel responded with retaliatory airstrikes on Palestinians in Gaza in a sharp escalation of clashes between the two sides.

2018-05-30.title: Dutch Official Says Russia Must Accept Blame for 2014 Attack on Jet
2018-05-30.abstract: Foreign Minister Stef Blok, whose country is leading the inquiry into the attack on the civilian plane that killed 298 people, urged Russia to “accept its responsibility.”

2018-05-30.title: Negotiations in Italy Push Euro to Fore, the Last Place Europe Wants It
2018-05-30.abstract: Europe seemed to be doing so well, but now Italy is making the euro the center of a populist debate. Would Italians really leave the eurozone?

2018-05-30.title: Battle to Stamp Out ISIS in Syria Gains New Momentum, but Threats Remain
2018-05-30.abstract: The return of top Syrian Kurdish commanders, a surge in French commandos, the arrival of Navy fighter jets and sleuthing by Iraqi spies have revived an effort that had stalled out.

2018-05-30.title: In South Sudan, a Never-Ending Hunger Season Puts Millions in Danger
2018-05-30.abstract: The Times went to South Sudan to document the extent of hunger as food grew scarce. This year is expected to be the worst yet, as millions potentially face acute malnutrition.

2018-05-30.title: When a Stranger Ruins Your Day
2018-05-30.abstract: Sometimes the city you call home can both charm you and break your heart.

2018-05-31.title: Race Against the Rains
2018-05-31.abstract: The race to rebuild the world’s largest refugee camp, where monsoon rains threaten flooding, landslides and disease.

2018-05-31.title: In Spain, Mariano Rajoy’s Government Veers Toward Collapse
2018-05-31.abstract: The issue for the prime minister is not the euro, as in Italy, but a corruption scandal that has damaged his party and spurred calls for his ouster.

2018-05-31.title: Crosses Go Up in Public Offices. It’s Culture, Bavaria Says, Not Religion.
2018-05-31.abstract: An order to hang crosses in government buildings has stirred the simmering culture wars since Germany opened its doors to more than a million migrants.

2018-05-31.title: Blurred Lines Between Journalists and What We Cover
2018-05-31.abstract: This week’s Australia Letter tackles the forces threatening to push journalism away from independence and candor.

2018-05-31.title: Don’t Call It an Embassy: U.S. Gives Its Taiwan Ties a $250 Million Upgrade
2018-05-31.abstract: The opening of a new American institute in Taiwan comes as the U.S. has slowly elevated its relationship with the island democracy, despite angering China.
